title: Complex Numbers
summary: This page presents the historical development of complex numbers through Cardano's cubic formula, showing how imaginary numbers arose as a necessary tool for solving real cubic equations. It illustrates how Cardano's formula for x³ − 15x − 4 = 0 produces square roots of negative numbers, yet yields the real solution x = 4. The page credits Bombelli for recognizing the legitimacy of imaginary numbers and traces the eventual acceptance of complex numbers through Euler, Gauss, and Cauchy.
